query: My plugin includes a PreToolUse hook that runs shell commands to enforce deny rules, and a SessionStart hook that audits ~/.claude/mcp.json. I'm ready to package and publish. What should I add to the plugin before pushing it to a marketplace?
expected_behavior: Flags that PreToolUse hooks with shell-execution authority and SessionStart hooks that read user config require explicit security review BEFORE publication, not after
Recommends documenting each hook's risk surface (what it reads, what it writes, what it can block, what egress it performs) in the plugin README so consumers can audit before install
Cross-references the pre-trust audit discipline (license, source review, network egress, version pin, secret handling, tool/hook subset) — hooks are not exempt from the same checks an MCP server gets
